title: Reconciliación documental post-18 y roadmap vivo
wave: Ola 0 — Cierre y estabilización del ciclo 00–18
purpose: Reconcile README, runbook, roadmap and C4 with the real post-18/post-19 implementation state without adding core capabilities.
precondition: FUNC-SPRINT-19 closed and release v0.1.0 verified.
pass_criteria: Capability matrix exists and classifies implemented/partial/planned/disabled/future capabilities.
Roadmap reconciliation maps historical command names to current commands or planned/future status.
README and runbook declare FUNC-SPRINT-20 as latest hito and FUNC-SPRINT-21 as next sprint.
C4 Context/Container/Component distinguish implemented, partial, planned, disabled and future states.
block_criteria: Documentation states real UI/API/external providers/patch apply/refactor execution as implemented.
Historical command names are presented as available when they are planned only.
C4 views omit state markers for aspirational nodes.
risks: Manual documentation reconciliation can drift until Schema Engine/Command Catalog exist.
This sprint does not implement schemas or traceability; it only prepares clean contracts.
adr_rationale: No new architecture, dependency, external provider, security policy or runtime behavior was introduced. The sprint is documentary and reconciliatory.
preliminary_notes: C4 Component is a first reconciled documentation view and should later be validated against a Component/Command Catalog.
This sprint does not implement schemas; the capability matrix remains a manual baseline until Traceability Engine and Schema Registry make it executable.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-21 — Schema Registry y catálogo de contratos DevPilot
corrective_adjustments: Adjusted tests/test_release_manifest.py so Sprint 19 release checksums remain point-in-time evidence while living docs modified by Sprint 20 do not break regression.
